name: Past Tense (mashita and ta)
summary: Form the polite past with -ました (mashita) and the plain past with -た (ta). Both say an action already happened.
explanation:
To put a verb into the past, the polite form is easy: take the -ます verb and change ます to ました. 食べます (tabemasu, eat) becomes 食べました (tabemashita, ate); 行きます (ikimasu, go) becomes 行きました (ikimashita, went). The polite past negative is ませんでした: 行きませんでした (ikimasen deshita, did not go).

The plain past, called the -た (ta) form, is used in casual speech and inside many grammar patterns. For ichidan verbs, replace る with た: 食べる becomes 食べた (tabeta). Godan verbs follow sound-based rules: 飲む becomes 飲んだ (nonda), 書く becomes 書いた (kaita), 待つ becomes 待った (matta). These euphonic changes take practice but are very regular once learned.

Nouns and na-adjectives form the past with でした (polite) or だった (plain): 学生でした (gakusei deshita, was a student). I-adjectives change differently, swapping い for かった, covered in the adjectives lesson.
pattern: [verb stem] + ました (polite)  /  plain past -た form

conjugation.polite past negative: 食べませんでした (tabemasen deshita) - did not eat
conjugation.noun past: 学生でした (gakusei deshita) - was a student

examples.native: 昨日寿司を食べました。(kinou sushi o tabemashita.)
先週京都へ行きました。(senshuu Kyouto e ikimashita.)
examples.en: I went to Kyoto last week.

examples.native: 彼は来ませんでした。(kare wa kimasen deshita.)
手紙を書きました。(tegami o kakimashita.)
去年は学生でした。(kyonen wa gakusei deshita.)
examples.en: Last year I was a student.

commonMistakes: Forming the polite past negative as ませんかった. It is ませんでした.
Applying ichidan rules to godan verbs in the plain past. 飲む becomes 飲んだ, not 飲た.
Using でした to make a verb past. でした is for nouns and na-adjectives, not -ます verbs.
Forgetting the euphonic sound changes in the godan -た form (kaku - kaita, matsu - matta).
Mixing plain and polite past forms in the same sentence.
tip: Polite past is mechanical: just turn ます into ました. The plain -た form takes more practice.
